title: Comments
description: Learn to leave notes in your code for yourself and others.

slides.title: What are Comments? 📝
slides.content:
Comments are text in your code that the C++ compiler completely ignores.

You use them to explain what your code does, making it easier for yourself and others to read later.

slides.title: Single-line Comments
slides.content:
To write a single-line comment, use two forward slashes `//`.

Everything after `//` on that line is ignored.
slides.codeSnippet:
// This is a single-line comment
std::cout << "Hello World!";

quizData.question: Which of these is a single-line comment in C++?

slides.title: Multi-line Comments 📚
slides.content:
If you have a lot to say, you can use multi-line comments.

They start with `/*` and end with `*/`.
